_doc: MCP tool allowlist + metadata. The mcp-server.mjs auto-discovers FLAGS from each adapter's tool_capabilities() at startup, but only exposes verbs explicitly declared here. Adding a new verb to MCP = 1 entry here + ensure adapter capabilities declare it; NO mcp-server.mjs edits.

<verb>.description: 1-3 sentence human-readable explanation (what MCP clients see in tools/list)
<verb>.required: optional array of required input property names (e.g. ['url'])
<verb>.oneOf: optional array of {required: [...]} objects for mutually-exclusive arg groups
<verb>.schemaExtras: optional per-property schema overrides (descriptions, enums, formats); merged onto auto-derived {type:'string'}
<verb>.globalProps: optional list of global props to add beyond auto-derived flags (defaults: ['site','tool']); set to ['site'] to skip the tool override prop
<verb>.excludeFlags: optional list of bash-verb flag NAMES (without --) to NOT expose via MCP (AP-7: secrets, --raw, --dry-run, --secret-stdin)
<verb>.scriptArgFlags: optional explicit ordering hint for argMap output

open.description: Open a URL via the routed browser adapter. Returns a summary JSON line with verb, tool, status, url, duration_ms. Auto-derives a post-condition check (url-include) so adapter-lies/redirect-to-login surface as oblivious_success in stats.jsonl.

snapshot.description: Capture an eN-indexed accessibility snapshot. Heavy YAML (> 2 KB) is persisted under captures/snapshots/ and returned via snapshot_path + n_refs in the summary; small payloads stay inline. With capture=true the full body is also archived under captures/NNN/.

click.description: Click an element by eN ref (preferred — stable across the session until the page mutates) or by CSS selector. Provide exactly one of ref or selector.

ref.description: eN ref from a prior browser_snapshot call

fill.description: Fill an input by eN ref or CSS selector with the given text. NOTE: this tool deliberately does NOT expose a 'secret' field — MCP has no stdin channel and putting secrets in tool arguments would land them in the request transcript. For secret values use scripts/browser-fill.sh directly with --secret-stdin (AP-7).

text.description: Plain-text value to type (NEVER pass secrets here)

ref.description: eN ref from a prior browser_snapshot call

selector.description: CSS selector (fallback when ref unavailable) — browser-fill.sh accepts this even though no adapter declares it in tool_capabilities

extract.description: Extract data via CSS selector or evaluated JS. selector returns concatenated text content of matched nodes; eval returns the JS expression result. --scrape multi-URL mode is intentionally NOT exposed via MCP (use scripts/browser-extract.sh --scrape directly).

selector.description: CSS selector to extract text from

eval.description: JS expression evaluated in page context

list-sites.description: List registered site profiles (no creds). Returns inline sites[] table. Auto-flips to TOON in MCP (40-60% smaller than JSON; see Phase 12 spec amendment).

format.description: Output envelope: 'toon' (default in MCP, ~40-60% smaller) or 'json'
